compiled_state.title: brainstorm skill: research spikes that may invalidate SC deferred to implementation -> SC may be unachievable
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Component
/dso:brainstorm (Phase 2 Approach + Spec Definition)

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run /dso:brainstorm on an epic with success criteria that depend on empirical feasibility (e.g., "pattern X is detected with confidence >= threshold on stack Y")
2. Brainstorm identifies that feasibility depends on a research spike (e.g., testing whether semgrep can reliably detect assertion-less tests in Ruby)
3. Brainstorm defers the spike to implementation with a note like "evaluate confidence during implementation"

## Expected Behavior
When a success criterion's achievability depends on an empirical result (tool capability, detection accuracy, engine coverage), brainstorm should conduct a lightweight feasibility spike during the session — at minimum a smoke test that confirms the approach is viable on each target stack. If the spike reveals that an SC is unachievable with the proposed approach, the SC can be revised while the user is present.

## Actual Behavior
Brainstorm accepts success criteria that assume feasibility without verification. Implementation discovers mid-sprint that a pattern cannot be detected on a specific stack, requiring SC revision without user context. This creates scope churn and wastes implementation effort on patterns that were never viable.

## Impact
SCs that assume tool capabilities without verification create false confidence in the epic spec. The brainstorm session is the optimal time for feasibility spikes because: (1) the user can immediately adjust scope, (2) the approach discussion is fresh in context, (3) research findings can inform the confidence mapping before it becomes a committed artifact.
